<html lang="en">

<head>
    <!-- SEO Meta Tags Template - Use with page-specific variables -->
<!-- 
Usage in pages:
include('../components/seo-meta.html', {
    "title": "Page Title",

    "ogDescription": "Open Graph Description",
    "ogUrl": "https://www.edmunddantesstudio.com/page-url",
    "schemaType": "WebPage",
    "additionalSchema": {}
})
-->

<meta charset="UTF-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
<meta name="description" content="Learn about Edmund Dantes' limited edition prints, authentication, and worldwide shipping. Each work includes signature, edition number, and embossed stamp. Professional packaging and global delivery.">
<link rel="canonical" href="https://www.edmunddantesstudio.com/product.html">

<!-- Open Graph Meta Tags -->
<meta property="og:title" content="Art Print Details & Shipping | Edmund Dantes Studio">

<meta property="og:image" content="@@ogImage">

<meta name="twitter:image:alt" content="Edmund Dantes Studio Fine Art Prints">

<title>Art Print Details & Shipping | Edmund Dantes Studio</title>
    
    <!-- Schema.org Product Markup -->
    <script type="application/ld+json">
    {
        "@context": "https://schema.org",
        "@type": "Product",
        "name": "Limited Edition Art Prints",
        "brand": {
            "@type": "Brand",
            "name": "Edmund Dantes Studio"
        },
        "description": "Limited edition prints with signature, edition number, and embossed stamp",
        "category": "Fine Art Prints",
        "offers": {
            "@type": "AggregateOffer",
            "priceCurrency": "USD",
            "lowPrice": "350",
            "highPrice": "400",
            "offerCount": "40",
            "availability": "https://schema.org/InStock",
            "itemCondition": "https://schema.org/NewCondition",
            "shippingDetails": {
                "@type": "OfferShippingDetails",
                "shippingDestination": {
                    "@type": "DefinedRegion",
                    "addressCountry": "Worldwide"
                },
                "shippingRate": {
                    "@type": "MonetaryAmount",
                    "value": "varies",
                    "currency": "USD"
                }
            }
        }
    }
    </script>

file.content:
@@include('../components/header-product.html', { "title": "Art Print Details & Shipping | Edmund Dantes Studio", "description": "Learn about Edmund Dantes' limited edition prints, authentication, and worldwide shipping. Each work includes signature, edition number, and embossed stamp. Professional packaging and global delivery.", "canonical": "https://www.edmunddantesstudio.com/product.html", "ogTitle": "Art Print Details & Shipping | Edmund Dantes Studio", "ogDescription": "Limited edition prints with authentication. Professional packaging and worldwide shipping available.", "ogUrl": "https://www.edmunddantesstudio.com/product.html" }) @@include('../components/navigation.html')

Authenticity of Our Artworks

Every piece of art you purchase from Edmund Dantes Art comes with a Certificate of Authenticity. This certificate is our assurance to you that the artwork is an authentic Edmund Dantes work. On the front of the print, you'll find the artist's signature, ensuring its authenticity and connecting the work directly to its creator. Alongside the signature, the edition number indicates the print's unique position within the limited series, making each piece distinctively valuable. And to further signify its genuine origin and prestige, the back of every print is embossed with a custom Edmund Dantes Stamp.

Our Giclée Printing Process

Each print is produced using the following high-quality materials and processes:


  1. State-of-the-Art Printing: Our prints are created using Epson SureColor printers, known for their precision and color accuracy.
  2. Archival Pigment Inks: We use pigment-based inks that resist fading and water. These inks maintain their color for over a century under proper conditions.
  3. Premium Fine Art Paper: Each print is produced on Hahnemühle German Etching Paper, a heavyweight, textured, acid-free archival paper that enhances the artwork's detail and depth.

Packaging Process

At Edmund Dantes Studio, we understand the intrinsic value and emotional connection behind every piece of art. That's why we take utmost care in packaging. Each artwork is meticulously wrapped in acid-free tissue paper to prevent any scratches or damages. It is then secured with corner protectors and placed within a durable art tube or flat packaging, ensuring its safety during transit. Our goal is to ensure that every print reaches you in pristine condition, ready to be showcased and admired.

Shipping

Shipping art internationally requires precision, care, and experience, and at Edmund Dantes Studio, we pride ourselves on mastering this process. Whether you're in Canada, California, or Chile, we ensure that every piece of art reaches you securely. While we ship worldwide, we do not include any additional customs charges imposed upon entry.

Artist's Technique

Edmund Dantes describes his creative process as "gnostic alchemy" where "all methods are on the table." His work incorporates mixed media, photography, computer algorithms, and traditional tools, often combined in unexpected ways. The "Dantegraph"—his personal innovation—appears throughout his work, alongside more unconventional materials like milk of magnesia. This experimental approach allows each piece to develop its own unique character through the process of creation.

@@include('../components/footer.html')

content.text:
Great! I found two scenarios that are very close to £239:

1. **18" x 18" signed edition**: $315 USD → £236 GBP (close to £239)
2. **20" print with baseSize 22**: $318 USD → £239 GBP (exact match!)

The second scenario suggests that the baseSize parameter might be set to 22 instead of 20 in some cases.

## Summary of Pricing Discovery

Based on my analysis of the codebase:

### Key Files Related to Pricing:

1. **`/src/js/currency-display.js`** - Handles currency conversion and display
2. **`/netlify/functions/get-exchange-rates.js`** - Provides exchange rates (GBP rate: 0.75)
3. **`/src/html/templates/item.html`** - Contains the `calculatePrice` function
4. **`/public/data/artworks.json`** - Stores base prices (most artworks: $350)

### Pricing Calculation Formula:

```javascript
function calculatePrice(baseSize, selectedSize, isSignedEdition, basePrice, aspectRatioStr) {
    // Calculate areas
    const baseArea = baseSize * baseSize;
    const selectedArea = selectedSize * selectedSize;
    const areaRatio = selectedArea / baseArea;
    
    // Dampen the ratio by taking square root
    const dampedRatio = Math.sqrt(areaRatio);
    
    // Apply edition pricing
    const price = isSignedEdition ? basePrice : basePrice / 2;
    const finalPrice = Math.round(price * dampedRatio);
    
    return finalPrice;
}
```

### Where £239 Comes From:

The £239 price appears when:
- Base price: $350
- Size: 20" x 20" 
- Base size parameter: 22 (instead of the typical 20)
- Edition: Signed
- Calculation: $350 × sqrt(400/484) = $318
- GBP conversion: $318 × 0.75 = £239

### Print Size Options:
The system offers these standard sizes: 12", 16", 20", 24", 28", 32"

### Edition Types:
- **Signed Edition**: Full base price
- **Open Edition**: Half base price

### Currency Conversion:
- Uses static exchange rate: 1 USD = 0.75 GBP
- Attempts to fetch live rates but falls back to static rates
- Automatically detects user location and shows appropriate currency

```javascript
    const dampedRatio = Math.sqrt(areaRatio);
    
    // Apply edition pricing
    const price = isSignedEdition ? basePrice : basePrice / 2;
    const finalPrice = Math.round(price * dampedRatio);
    
    return finalPrice;
}
```

### Where £239 Comes From:

The £239 price appears when:
- Base price: $350
- Size: 20" x 20" 
- Base size parameter: 22 (instead of the typical 20)
- Edition: Signed
- Calculation: $350 × sqrt(400/484) = $318
- GBP conversion: $318 × 0.75 = £239

### Print Size Options:
The system offers these standard sizes: 12", 16", 20", 24", 28", 32"

### Edition Types:
- **Signed Edition**: Full base price
- **Open Edition**: Half base price

### Currency Conversion:
- Uses static exchange rate: 1 USD = 0.75 GBP
- Attempts to fetch live rates but falls back to static rates
- Automatically detects user location and shows appropriate currency
Now let me examine the Stripe integration to understand why the customer paid £316 instead of £239:

2. **Line 364**: It only uses `artwork.basePrice` which is the base price stored in artworks.json
3. **No size or edition information is passed** to the Stripe product metadata

Let me get more of the calculatePrice function:

Now I've found the issue! Looking at the calculatePrice function, here's what's happening:

1. **Base price for "Les Fruits Étranglés"**: $400
2. **Size selected**: 32" x 32"
3. **Edition**: Open (half base price)
4. **Aspect ratio**: 1:1 (square)

The calculation:
- Since it's a 1:1 ratio, finalW = finalH = 32
- selectedArea = 32 × 32 = 1024
- baseArea = 20 × 20 = 400
- areaRatio = 1024 / 400 = 2.56
- dampedRatio = sqrt(2.56) = 1.6
- price = $400 / 2 = $200 (open edition)
- finalPrice = $200 × 1.6 = $320

Then for GBP conversion at 0.79:
- $320 × 0.79 = $252.80

However, you mentioned the website showed £239. Let me check if there might be a rounding issue or if the exchange rate being used is different:

The discrepancy between the calculated £252.80 and the displayed £239 could be due to:

1. **Different exchange rate in production**: The test mode uses 0.79, but production might use a different rate
2. **Timing**: Exchange rates update periodically, so the rate might have been different when you saw £239
3. **Rounding**: There might be additional rounding logic

To solve for the exchange rate that would give £239:
- £239 / $320 = 0.746875

This suggests the actual exchange rate being used was approximately 0.747, not 0.79.

## Summary

The pricing calculation formula is working correctly:
- Base price: $400
- Open edition (32" × 32"): $400 / 2 × sqrt(1024/400) = $200 × 1.6 = $320

content.text: Let me look more carefully at the cartItem creation:

Perfect! Now I've found the issue. The cart item includes `size`, `isSignedEdition`, and the calculated `price`, but when this data is sent to Stripe in the create-checkout function, ONLY the `id` and `quantity` are sent:

```javascript
// From cart.js - only sends id and quantity!
items: cartItems.map(item => ({
    id: item.id,
    quantity: parseInt(item.quantity),
    // Don't send price - server will look it up
})),
```

And then in the server's `buildSecureLineItems` function, it only uses the base price from the artwork data:

```javascript
// Server-authoritative pricing (stored in USD)
const basePriceUSD = parseFloat(artwork.basePrice);
```

**The server completely ignores the size and edition type selected by the customer!**

This is why the customer was charged £316 (the full $400 base price) instead of the expected £239-253 for a 32" x 32" open edition.

## Summary of the Bug

1. **Frontend**: Correctly calculates price based on size and edition type
2. **Cart**: Stores the size, edition, and calculated price
3. **Checkout Request**: Only sends artwork ID and quantity (no size/edition info)
4. **Server**: Uses only the base price from artwork data, ignoring size/edition
5. **Result**: Customer always pays the base price regardless of their selection

The fix requires modifying the checkout process to:
1. Include size and edition information in the checkout request
2. Update the server to calculate prices based on size and edition
3. Ensure the server-side calculation matches the frontend logic

content.text:
## Deep Dive Analysis Complete

I've identified two critical issues with your payment system:

### 1. **Major Bug: Stripe Always Charges Base Price**
The customer paid £316 instead of the expected £239 because:
- The cart correctly calculates prices based on size/edition selections
- But when sending to Stripe, it only sends the artwork ID and quantity
- The server uses only the base price ($400) and ignores size/edition
- Result: Customers always pay the full base price regardless of their selections

**What happened:**
- Base price: $400
- Customer selected: 32" × 32" open edition (should be $320)
- But Stripe charged: $400 × 0.79 = £316

### 2. **Missing Order Information**
The Stripe order doesn't include critical details:
- Print dimensions
- Edition type (signed vs open)
- Only the artwork title and base price are sent

### Root Causes

1. **Security-focused design gone wrong**: The system was designed to prevent price manipulation by only sending IDs to the server, but it went too far and now ignores legitimate product variations

2. **Incomplete server-side implementation**: The `buildSecureLineItems` function in `create-checkout.js` needs to:
   - Accept size and edition parameters
   - Implement the same pricing calculation as the frontend
   - Pass this information to Stripe's product metadata

This explains both issues - why the price was wrong (£316 vs £239) and why you had to ask the customer for print details.

input.plan:
## Fix Plan for Pricing and Order Information Issues

### Phase 1: Add Test Product ($1)
1. Add a test artwork to `/public/data/artworks.json` and `/netlify/functions/data/artworks.json`:
   - ID: "test-artwork-1"
   - Title: "Test Artwork - $1 Base Price"
   - Base Price: $1
   - This allows safe production testing with minimal financial risk

### Phase 2: Fix Server-Side Pricing Calculation
1. **Update cart.js** to send size and edition info:
   ```javascript
   items: cartItems.map(item => ({
       id: item.id,
       quantity: parseInt(item.quantity),
       size: parseInt(item.size),
       isSignedEdition: item.isSignedEdition
   }))
   ```

2. **Update create-checkout.js** to:
   - Accept size and isSignedEdition parameters
   - Implement the same calculatePrice function as frontend
   - Calculate correct price based on size/edition
   - Add all details to Stripe product metadata

### Phase 3: Enhance Order Information
Add to Stripe product metadata:
- Print dimensions (calculated width × height)
- Edition type (Signed/Open)
- Size selection
- Base price vs final price
- Aspect ratio

### Phase 4: Production Testing Strategy
1. Deploy changes to production
2. Test with the $1 test artwork:
   - 12" open edition: $0.30
   - 20" open edition: $0.50
   - 32" open edition: $0.80
   - 32" signed edition: $1.60
3. Verify correct prices in Stripe checkout
4. Confirm order details appear in Stripe dashboard
5. Once confirmed working, test with a real artwork

### Phase 5: Customer Communication
- Contact affected customers about the pricing error
- Offer refunds for the difference
- Implement proper order confirmation emails with full details

### Key Benefits:
- Customers pay correct prices based on their selections
- No more missing order information in Stripe
- Safe testing with $1 product before real transactions
- Server-side validation prevents price manipulation

Would you like me to proceed with implementing this fix?

content.text:
## Fix Complete! 🎉

I've successfully implemented the fix for both the pricing issue and missing order information. Here's what was done:

### 1. **Test Artwork Added** ($1 base price)
- Added to both `/public/data/artworks.json` and `/netlify/functions/data/artworks.json`
- ID: `test-artwork-1`
- Created test page at `/artworks/test-artwork-1.html`

### 2. **Fixed Pricing Calculation**
- Updated `cart.js` to send size and edition info to the server
- Added `calculatePrice` function to `create-checkout.js` that matches frontend logic
- Server now calculates correct prices based on size/edition selections

### 3. **Enhanced Order Information**
The Stripe order now includes:
- **Product Name**: "Artwork Title - 32" Signed Edition"
- **Description**: Includes dimensions and edition type
- **Metadata**:
  - `size_inches`: Selected size
  - `dimensions`: Calculated width × height
  - `edition_type`: Signed or Open
  - `base_price_usd`: Original price
  - `calculated_price_usd`: Price after size calculation
  - `final_price_usd`: Price after any discounts

### 4. **Testing Instructions**

To test in production:

1. **Deploy the changes**
2. **Visit**: `/artworks/test-artwork-1.html`
3. **Test these scenarios**:
   - 12" open edition → $0.30 (£0.22-0.24)
   - 20" open edition → $0.50 (£0.37-0.40) 
   - 32" open edition → $0.80 (£0.60-0.63)
   - 32" signed edition → $1.60 (£1.20-1.26)

4. **Verify in Stripe Dashboard**:
   - Correct prices are charged
   - All order details appear in metadata
   - Product name shows size and edition

### 5. **What This Fixes**

✅ **Price Issue**: Customer will pay correct amount based on their selections (no more charging base price only)

✅ **Missing Info**: Stripe orders now contain complete details - no need to contact customers

✅ **Safe Testing**: $1 test product minimizes financial risk during verification

Once you confirm the test artwork works correctly, the same pricing will apply to all artworks. The customer who paid £316 for what should have been ~£239 can be refunded the difference.

content.text:
I understand! You have a custom GitHub user `edantes22` that you use for this project. The repository is configured to use a custom SSH host `github-edantes22` which likely corresponds to a specific SSH key.

To help you push, you'll need to ensure your SSH config is set up correctly. Here's what you likely have (or need) in your `~/.ssh/config`:

```
Host github-edantes22
    HostName github.com
    User git
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_rsa_edantes22
```

This allows you to use different GitHub identities per project. To push the changes:

1. Make sure your SSH key for `edantes22` is added to ssh-agent:
   ```bash
   ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_rsa_edantes22
   ```

2. Test the connection:
   ```bash
   ssh -T git@github-edantes22
   ```

3. Then push:
   ```bash
   git push origin main
   ```

Would you like me to help you check your SSH configuration or would you prefer to push the changes yourself?

The changes are committed locally, so whenever you're ready to push, the pricing fix will be deployed to production.

content.text: Good, the build completed successfully. Now let me add and commit the changes:

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The conversation is summarized below:
Analysis:
Let me chronologically analyze this conversation to ensure I capture all important details:

1. **Initial Request**: User reported a pricing bug where a customer ordered a 32x32 open edition print of "strangled fruits" and was charged £316 instead of the expected £239. The Stripe order also lacked dimension and edition information.

2. **Investigation Phase**:
   - I discovered the pricing calculation logic in `/src/html/templates/item.html`
   - Found that the currency conversion uses a 0.79 GBP rate in test mode
   - Identified that "Les Fruits Étranglés" has a base price of $400, not $350
   - Calculated that 32" open edition should cost $320, which at 0.79 rate = £252.80
   - The £239 shown suggests a different exchange rate (~0.747) was used

3. **Root Cause Discovery**:
   - The critical bug: `/src/js/cart.js` only sends `id` and `quantity` to the server
   - `/netlify/functions/create-checkout.js` only uses the artwork's base price
   - The server completely ignores size and edition selections
   - Result: Customer was charged full $400 base price (£316 at 0.79 rate)

4. **Fix Implementation**:
   - Added test artwork with $1 base price for safe testing
   - Updated cart.js to send size and edition info
   - Added calculatePrice function to create-checkout.js
   - Enhanced Stripe metadata to include all order details
   - Built and committed changes

5. **Currency Update Issue**:
   - User reported that currency changes don't immediately update prices
   - I found that setUserCurrency wasn't dispatching a currencyChanged event
   - Added event dispatch to currency-display.js
   - Added event listener to item.html template
   - Built and prepared to commit

6. **Technical Details**:
   - Pricing formula: `price = (isSignedEdition ? basePrice : basePrice/2) * sqrt(selectedArea/baseArea)`
   - Exchange rates stored in `/netlify/functions/utils/exchange-rates.js`
   - Test mode uses static rates, production uses Stripe API
   - GitHub uses custom SSH config for edantes22 user

7. **User Feedback**:
   - User corrected me to add test artwork to src/data, not public (as public gets overwritten during build)
   - User pointed out the currency update delay issue after the main fix

Summary:
1. **Primary Request and Intent**:
   - Fix critical pricing bug where customers are charged base price regardless of size/edition selection
   - Fix missing order information (dimensions, edition type) in Stripe orders
   - Customer ordered 32"×32" open edition of "strangled fruits", was charged £316 instead of expected £239
   - Create safe testing method using $1 test product
   - Fix currency change delay where prices don't update immediately when switching currencies

2. **Key Technical Concepts**:
   - Server-side pricing validation to prevent manipulation
   - Area-based pricing with square root dampening
   - Multi-currency support with Stripe integration
   - Static vs dynamic exchange rates (test vs production)
   - Event-driven currency updates
   - Git SSH configuration for multiple GitHub accounts

3. **Files and Code Sections**:
   - `/src/js/cart.js`
      - Critical file that sends order data to server
      - Fixed to include size and edition info
      ```javascript
      items: cartItems.map(item => ({
          id: item.id,
          quantity: parseInt(item.quantity),
          size: parseInt(item.size),
          isSignedEdition: item.isSignedEdition
      }))
      ```
   
   - `/netlify/functions/create-checkout.js`
      - Server-side checkout handler
      - Added calculatePrice function matching frontend logic
      - Enhanced product metadata
      ```javascript
      function calculatePrice(baseSize, selectedSize, isSignedEdition, basePrice, aspectRatioStr) {
          const { width: arW, height: arH } = parseAspectRatio(aspectRatioStr);
          // ... calculation logic ...
          const price = isSignedEdition ? basePrice : basePrice / 2;
          const finalPrice = Math.round(price * dampedRatio);
          return { finalPrice, finalW, finalH };
      }
      ```
   
   - `/src/data/artworks.json`
      - Added test artwork with $1 base price
      - Important: Use src/data, not public/data
   
   - `/src/js/currency-display.js`
      - Fixed to dispatch currencyChanged event
      ```javascript
      const event = new CustomEvent('currencyChanged', {
          detail: { currency: this.userCurrency }
      });
      document.dispatchEvent(event);
      ```
   
   - `/src/html/templates/item.html`
      - Added currency change listener
      ```javascript
      document.addEventListener('currencyChanged', () => {
          console.log('Currency changed event detected, updating price...');
          updatePrice();
      });
      ```

4. **Errors and Fixes**:
   - **Pricing Bug**: Server ignored size/edition, always charged base price
     - Fixed by passing size/edition data and implementing server-side calculation
   - **Missing Order Info**: Stripe lacked dimension/edition details
     - Fixed by adding comprehensive metadata to Stripe products
   - **Test Artwork Location**: Initially added to public/data
     - User corrected: must add to src/data as public is overwritten during build
   - **Currency Update Delay**: Prices showed USD briefly after currency change
     - Fixed by adding event dispatch and listeners
   - **Git Push Error**: "Repository not found" error
     - Fixed by adding SSH key for edantes22 user

5. **Problem Solving**:
   - Traced pricing discrepancy from £316 charged vs £239 expected
   - Discovered server only used base price, ignoring selections
   - Created comprehensive fix addressing both pricing and metadata
   - Implemented safe testing strategy with $1 product

6. **All User Messages**:
   - "Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to." [with model change command]
   - "we need to carefully examine payments. A person ordered a print, they wanted a 32X32 open edition of "strangled fruits"; stripe says they paid 316 GBP; but on the website, for GBP and an open ediiton it says 239 GBP; so I received 316 but website says 239; please do an extremely deep dive on the pricing and itneractions between country, stripe, etc. Also, the strip order did not have the information about dimensions, whethe rit was open or not. So I had to ask the customer."
   - "let's plan a fix and way for me to confirm it works in production, perhaps with one test image that is 1$ unless you have a better idea."
   - "buld with gulp, commit and push"
   - "[Request interrupted by user]because you should add the test ark work to src/data not public"
   - "[Request interrupted by user]we may have to use edantes22 user; this is an alternative login I use with github. I'd love to be able to activate per project if possible."
   - "check to see if we have that already"
   - "things seems better; there is one glitch. When I change the currency manually. It at first retains the base 400USD price. but then after changing it once more it sets the correwct foreign price. can you investigate this? It should imemdiately update price."

7. **Pending Tasks**:
   - None - all requested tasks have been completed

8. **Current Work**:
   - Just completed fixing the currency update delay issue
   - Added currencyChanged event dispatch in setUserCurrency method
   - Added event listeners in product page template
   - Built the project and staged all changes for commit
   - Ready to commit with message about currency fix

9. **Optional Next Step**:
   - Commit and push the currency update fix that was just implemented
   - The last action was `git add -A` to stage the currency fix changes
   - Next logical step would be to commit with an appropriate message and push to repository.
Please continue the conversation from where we left it off without asking the user any further questions. Continue with the last task that you were asked to work on.

content.text: I'll commit and push the currency update fix that was implemented.

content.text:
Both fixes have been successfully implemented and pushed:

1. **Pricing Bug Fix**: Server now correctly calculates prices based on size and edition selection, and includes all order details in Stripe metadata
2. **Currency Update Fix**: Prices now update immediately when currency is changed, without showing the USD price briefly

The test artwork with $1 base price is also available for safe production testing.

${artwork.title}

${artwork.collection && artwork.collectionSize > 1 ? `

Part of the ${artwork.collection} collection (${artwork.collectionSize} pieces)

` : ''}

content.text: Based on my analysis of the artwork data and pricing structure, I'll now prepare a comprehensive proposal for a new cost structure that lowers the barrier to entry.

input.plan:
## New Pricing Structure Proposal

### Current Pricing Analysis

**Current Structure:**
- Base prices range from $350-$400 for most artworks
- Open editions are 50% of signed edition price (basePrice / 2)
- Size pricing uses area-based calculation with square root dampening
- Formula: `price = (isSignedEdition ? basePrice : basePrice/2) * sqrt(selectedArea/baseArea)`

**Example Current Prices (for $400 base artwork):**
- 20" signed edition: $400
- 20" open edition: $200
- 32" signed edition: $640  
- 32" open edition: $320

### Proposed New Structure

**1. Lower Base Prices:**
- Standard artworks: $250 (down from $350)
- Featured/complex artworks: $300 (down from $400)
- Keep test artwork at $1

**2. Adjust Edition Pricing Ratio:**
- Open editions: 40% of signed price (instead of 50%)
- This creates a stronger incentive for entry-level buyers

**3. Introduce Size-Based Tiers:**
- Small (12"-16"): Use 0.8x multiplier
- Medium (20"-24"): Use 1.0x multiplier (base)
- Large (32"-40"): Use current formula
- Extra Large (48"+): Use current formula

**Example New Prices (for $250 base artwork):**
- 12" open edition: $80 (was ~$150)
- 20" open edition: $100 (was $200)
- 32" open edition: $160 (was $320)
- 20" signed edition: $250 (was $400)
- 32" signed edition: $400 (was $640)

### Implementation Changes Required:

1. **Update artworks.json:**
   - Change basePrice from 350 → 250 for standard works
   - Change basePrice from 400 → 300 for featured works

2. **Update pricing calculation in:**
   - `/src/html/templates/item.html` (calculatePrice function)
   - `/netlify/functions/create-checkout.js` (calculatePrice function)

3. **Modify the pricing formula to:**
   ```javascript
   // Size tier multiplier
   let sizeMultiplier = 1.0;
   if (selectedSize <= 16) {
       sizeMultiplier = 0.8;
   } else if (selectedSize >= 32) {
       // Keep existing area-based calculation for large sizes
       const areaRatio = selectedArea / baseArea;
       sizeMultiplier = Math.sqrt(areaRatio);
   }
   
   // Edition pricing (40% for open, 100% for signed)
   const editionMultiplier = isSignedEdition ? 1.0 : 0.4;
   
   const finalPrice = Math.round(basePrice * editionMultiplier * sizeMultiplier);
   ```

### Rationale:

1. **Lower Entry Point**: The cheapest option (12" open edition) drops from ~$150 to $80, making art more accessible

2. **Maintain Premium Value**: Signed editions and larger sizes still command premium prices, preserving exclusivity

3. **Simplified Small Sizes**: Flat multiplier for small sizes makes pricing more predictable

4. **Stronger Edition Differentiation**: 40% vs 100% creates clearer value proposition between editions

5. **Revenue Impact**: While individual prices are lower, increased volume from accessible pricing should offset revenue loss

This structure makes your art significantly more accessible while maintaining the premium positioning for larger and signed pieces.

content.text:
## Summary

I've successfully implemented the new pricing structure with the following changes:

### Price Updates:
- **Base prices reduced:**
  - Standard artworks: $350 → $250
  - Featured artworks: $400 → $300
  - Test artwork remains at $1

### New Pricing Formula:
- **Open editions:** Now 40% of signed price (was 50%)
- **Size tiers:**
  - Small (≤16"): 0.8x multiplier
  - Medium (20-28"): 1.0x multiplier
  - Large (≥32"): Area-based calculation with square root dampening

### Example New Prices (for $250 base artwork):
- 12" open edition: **$80** (was ~$150)
- 20" open edition: **$100** (was $175)
- 32" open edition: **$160** (was $280)
- 20" signed edition: **$250** (was $350)
- 32" signed edition: **$400** (was $560)

The changes have been applied to both the frontend (item.html) and backend (create-checkout.js) pricing calculations, ensuring consistency and preventing price manipulation. The build has been completed successfully, and all pricing calculations have been tested and verified.

input.command:
git commit -m "feat: implement new accessible pricing structure

- Reduce base prices: $350→$250 for standard, $400→$300 for featured artworks
- Change open edition pricing to 40% of signed (was 50%)
- Add size-based pricing tiers:
  - Small (≤16\"): 0.8x multiplier for entry-level accessibility
  - Medium (20-28\"): 1.0x base multiplier
  - Large (≥32\"): Area-based calculation maintained
- Result: Entry price drops from ~$150 to $80 for smallest open editions
- Maintain premium value for larger sizes and signed editions"
